summary: Iter 292: Verified centralized multichannel notification system (bell + WhatsApp + Email) and new WhatsApp send-log endpoint. Fixed one CRITICAL bug found on first probe (GET /api/super-admin/whatsapp/messages returned HTTP 500 due to undefined get_database()). After fix: 10/10 new iter292 tests pass and 20/20 regression tests (shift/expense isolation + iter288 mandatory shift_id) still pass — total 30/30.

minor.issue: Was raising HTTP 500 (NameError: get_database) — fixed in test iteration by removing the stray db=get_database() line; module-level 'db' from server.py is already usable via wildcard import.

verification_details.wa_messages_endpoint: GET /api/super-admin/whatsapp/messages -> 200 with keys {messages, counts:{total,sent,failed}}. Filters ?status=sent and ?status=failed return only matching rows. Requires super_admin auth (401 without token). Ceiling limit=200.
verification_details.wa_test_logs_row: POST /api/super-admin/whatsapp/test with phone +201000000000 -> 200 {success:false,error:'not_connected'} AND a new row appears in wa_messages within ~1.5s with purpose='test', status='failed', valid id/phone/message_preview/message_length/sent_at fields. message_preview capped at 200 chars.
verification_details.notify_owner_multichannel: Direct import + invocation from server.py. Returns dict {bell:True, whatsapp:False, email:False} — WhatsApp fails gracefully (service reports not_connected / no owner phone), Email fails gracefully (SMTP password not configured). bell=True always. DB row lands in db.notifications with severity/category/metadata and is visible via GET /api/super-admin/notifications.
verification_details.forgotten_shift_alert: Seeded shift {status:open, started_at:-14h, alerted_forgotten:false}. Manually invoked _alert_forgotten_open_shifts(). Verified db.notifications row created with category='shift', severity='warning', metadata.shift_id=<seed> AND shift.alerted_forgotten flipped to true.
verification_details.auth_gate: GET /api/super-admin/whatsapp/messages without Authorization returns 401 with Arabic detail (per verify_super_admin dependency).
verification_details.regression: 20/20 pass across: test_ahmed_shift_close_regression (2), test_lazy_shift_and_daily_isolation (4), test_expense_shift_isolation (4), test_iter288_shift_id_mandatory (10). /api/health -> 200 ok. /api/super-admin/whatsapp/status -> 200 with connected:false + qr:base64 + error:null.

action_items: Confirm the one-line fix applied (removed 'db = get_database()' inside wa_messages_list at /app/backend/routes/super_admin_routes.py). No other changes made to production code.
To enable end-to-end WhatsApp delivery to owner, add a phone (E.164) to the super_admin/owner user document OR set OWNER_ALERT_PHONE env var. Current graceful-degradation is already correct.
To enable end-to-end email delivery to Moataz0111@gmail.com, configure SMTP password via PUT /api/system/email-settings (recovery emails list already contains Moataz0111@gmail.com).

Optional cleanup: after test run, wa_messages counts=1 failed will grow with each pytest run (test_wa_test_logs_row leaves rows behind). Consider adding a delete-by-purpose maintenance endpoint or auto-purge after N days.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL FIX APPLIED: /app/backend/routes/super_admin_routes.py wa_messages_list had 'db = get_database()' but get_database is not imported here and not re-exported by server.py. This caused 100% HTTP 500 on the endpoint. Removed the stray line — the wildcard 'from server import *' already brings 'db' into scope, matching every other endpoint in this file.

whatsapp_free._log_message spins up a NEW AsyncIOMotorClient per send call to sidestep the server↔whatsapp_free circular import. Works, but creates a client per send — for high-volume sends prefer a lazy module-level singleton created on first call. Not blocking.

notify_owner_multichannel logs the WhatsApp payload with the FULL title+body via _wa_free.send_message (which stores first 200 chars in wa_messages.message_preview). This is fine for owner_shift/owner_system category but be careful never to route OTP payloads through this multichannel helper, otherwise OTP first-200-chars would land in the log. The dedicated OTP path already uses purpose='otp' — keep it separate.
server.py OWNER_RECOVERY_EMAILS default already includes Moataz0111@gmail.com so multichannel email will target that inbox once SMTP is configured — good.

_alert_forgotten_open_shifts correctly sets alerted_forgotten=true after sending, so a broken WhatsApp/Email leg will not cause repeat spamming on next cron tick. Bell entry is written unconditionally before the flag flip.
server.py is 17,109 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. The multichannel helper (~90 lines) could live in a small notifications_service.py alongside send_system_email. Not blocking.

Import cost: 'from server import *' in super_admin_routes.py pulls hundreds of symbols and is fragile — new endpoints must remember to reference module-level 'db', not call unimported helpers. The bug fixed above is a direct consequence. Consider an explicit 'from server import db, ...' at the top.
test_credentials: Super Admin: owner@maestroegp.com / owner123 / secret_key=271018 — verified. Admin: admin@maestroegp.com / admin123 (tenant=default). wa_service token from env, backend auto-uses.
seed_data_creation: test_forgotten_shift_alert_creates_bell seeds+cleans one shifts row and one notifications row per run. test_notify_multichannel_writes_bell_and_returns_dict + test_notify_multichannel_visible_via_api leave two TEST_iter292_* notifications behind (small, harmless). test_wa_test_logs_row leaves 1 wa_messages row per run (purpose=test, phone +201000000000).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter292 verified 3-channel notify (bell/WhatsApp/Email) with graceful degradation and new WhatsApp send-log endpoint. All expected behaviors confirmed. One prod bug fixed (get_database NameError in wa_messages_list). Regression suite intact (20/20). WhatsApp is 'connected:false' at wa_service level right now so test_wa_test_logs_row records status=failed rows — this is EXPECTED and matches user context. Email SMTP password intentionally missing — email leg returns False; expected. If future testing agents need genuine WhatsApp/Email delivery to Moataz0111@gmail.com, first pair the WA service (POST /api/super-admin/whatsapp/pair) and configure SMTP password (PUT /api/system/email-settings).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of the observed 500 on GET /api/super-admin/whatsapp/messages: new endpoint used 'db = get_database()' but get_database is only defined in routes/shared.py and never imported in super_admin_routes.py (which relies on 'from server import *' — server.py does not re-export get_database). The local assignment therefore raised NameError before the DB was touched. Fix: dropped the stray line; wildcard-imported 'db' from server is already the shared AsyncIOMotorDatabase used by every other endpoint in the file. Regression-safe (30/30). Reproduction: hit GET /api/super-admin/whatsapp/messages with a valid super_admin token — before fix HTTP 500, after fix HTTP 200 with {messages, counts}.
